title: DAX, FTSE 100 Forecast: 2 Trades to Watch
author: Fiona Cincotta
description: The DAX has reached record highs, benefiting from a rotation out of US tech into European cyclicals and industrials, which offer better valuations. The FTSE 100 has broken above its symmetrical triangle pattern with M&A activity in focus, including EasyJet's takeover by Castlelake and ITV's sale of its media business to Sky. Both indices are well-positioned as investors question the sustainability of the AI-driven rally and seek value in European equities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Agreed to sell media and entertainment business to Sky for £1.6 billion, creating a larger UK-focused streaming platform better positioned to compete with global rivals like Netflix and Prime Video.

Accepted takeover offer from Castlelake after months of speculation, ending uncertainty. The acquisition follows weakness in share price due to higher fuel costs and softer travel demand, providing a positive resolution for shareholders.
Mentioned as starting earnings season this week; investors will watch for signs that corporate earnings can justify elevated equity valuations, but no specific sentiment indicated.
Mentioned as starting earnings season this week; investors will watch for signs that corporate earnings can justify elevated equity valuations, but no specific sentiment indicated.

title: Q2 Earnings Season Is About to Kick Off. These 5 Reports Will Set the Market's Tone.
author: Daniel Sparks
description: Q2 earnings season begins with five key reports that will determine whether the market's rally is supported by actual earnings growth. Delta Air Lines (July 10) will signal consumer spending on travel, JPMorgan Chase (July 14) will reveal credit quality and economic health, Netflix (July 16) will show if pricing power holds amid competition, Taiwan Semiconductor (July 16) will indicate AI chip demand trends, and Tesla (July 22) will demonstrate EV demand sustainability. Together, these reports will answer whether current stock valuations are justified.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Delta is positioned as an early indicator of consumer discretionary spending and travel demand. While it has beaten profit forecasts for four quarters, the article focuses on forward guidance rather than making a bullish or bearish case.

As the largest U.S. bank, JPMorgan's report is presented as a critical economic barometer for credit quality and loan demand. The article treats it as an important data point without expressing optimism or pessimism about outcomes.

Netflix is highlighted for its pricing power and advertising growth, but the article questions whether these advantages can hold as competition intensifies. The tone is inquisitive rather than bullish or bearish.

The article identifies TSM as 'the most important of the five' reports and notes that a confident forecast could 'help calm chip-stock nerves.' This suggests the author views a positive TSM report as potentially beneficial for the market.

Tesla's report is framed as a test of whether Q2's record deliveries represent sustainable demand or merely a pull-forward effect. The article presents this as an open question without expressing conviction either way.

title: 3 EV Stocks To Buy This Month
author: Leo Sun
description: The article recommends three EV stocks as buying opportunities: Rivian, expected to boost deliveries with its cheaper R2 SUV; BYD, which surpassed Tesla as the world's largest EV maker with strong growth prospects; and Joby Aviation, developing electric aircraft with major backing from Toyota, Delta, and Uber, pending FAA certification.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Production challenges are being addressed with the launch of the cheaper R2 SUV starting at $57,990, expected to expand addressable market and boost deliveries to 62,000-67,000 vehicles this year. Revenue projected to more than triple by 2028 with positive EBITDA expected, trading at attractive 3x sales valuation.

Surpassed Tesla as world's largest EV maker with 4.6 million vehicles sold in 2025. Competitive advantages include proprietary LFP batteries and vertically integrated supply chain. Expanding internationally and analysts expect 13% revenue CAGR and 23% EPS CAGR through 2028, trading at reasonable 14x earnings.

Strong backing from major investors (Toyota, Delta, Uber) with clear commercialization path. S4 eVTOL aircraft has superior technical specifications. FAA certification could significantly boost sales, with analysts projecting revenue to rise nearly ninefold by 2028. Massive market potential with 30,000 eVTOLs projected by 2045.

Strong backing from major investors (Toyota, Delta, Uber) with clear commercialization path. S4 eVTOL aircraft has superior technical specifications. FAA certification could significantly boost sales, with analysts projecting revenue to rise nearly ninefold by 2028. Massive market potential with 30,000 eVTOLs projected by 2045.
Mentioned as context that BYD surpassed Tesla as the world's largest EV maker by annual vehicle sales in 2025, indicating competitive pressure in the EV market.
Mentioned as a major investor and manufacturing partner for Joby Aviation's commercial eVTOLs, supporting the company's commercialization efforts.
Mentioned as a customer planning to use Joby's S4 for airport-to-home air taxi services, representing a key commercial application.
Mentioned as a partner integrating Joby's flights into its new Uber Air platform, supporting the eVTOL commercialization ecosystem.

title: Why Delek Holdings Rallied Today
author: Billy Duberstein
description: Delek U.S. stock rallied 6% on Thursday following a fire at the Trainer refinery in Pennsylvania, owned by Delta Air Lines' subsidiary Monroe Energy. The incident is expected to tighten jet fuel supply and boost margins for competing refiners like Delek, which has a high percentage of revenue from jet fuel. The rally comes as investors had anticipated margin compression following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, but the refinery outage could offset that decline.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rallied 6% due to a competitor's refinery fire reducing jet fuel supply, which should increase margins and prices for Delek. The company is positioned to benefit from supply tightening and is trading at a valuation that appears attractive relative to its Delek Logistics stake.

Delta's wholly-owned Trainer refinery suffered a fire and will be offline for repairs, disrupting its competitive advantage in jet fuel supply and potentially impacting its refining operations and profitability during the recovery period.
As a subsidiary of Delek U.S. (63% stake), it benefits indirectly from the improved refining margins and supply tightening caused by the competitor's refinery outage.

title: The Strait of Hormuz Is Open: Time to Buy Airline Stocks?
author: Emma Newbery
description: Following a U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding, the Strait of Hormuz has reopened and oil prices have fallen 20% over the past month. While airline stocks have already recovered from conflict-related losses, investors should focus on individual airline fundamentals rather than geopolitical events. Delta Air Lines demonstrates resilience through premium customer focus and an oil refinery hedge, while American Airlines pursues a turnaround strategy despite higher debt levels.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Delta demonstrated resilience with 21% year-to-date gains, increased dividend by 15%, owns an oil refinery to offset fuel costs, generates 60%+ revenue from premium customers, and has strong leadership. Lower debt ($13.5B) provides financial flexibility.

American Airlines is pursuing strategic improvements including premium customer focus and expanded credit card partnerships, but faces significant headwinds from high debt ($34.7B), higher net losses than Delta, and underperformance versus industry peers. Presents potential opportunity but with considerable risks.

The ETF is trading higher than when the conflict started, reflecting market pricing in of geopolitical resolution. However, the article cautions against investing based on Strait of Hormuz developments, suggesting limited upside from this specific catalyst.
